rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Tuli, Bharadwaj, and Kohli (2010) to justify how it operationalizes the intangible asset emphasis variable in its propensity-score matching setup. This is a direct methodological reference tied to model construction, not a generic background citation. While the cited paper is not central to the focal hypotheses, it materially informs an identification/control decision in the empirical design.
relation_description: The citing study applies a measurement approach from Tuli et al. (2010) for intangible asset emphasis, adapting it to firm-level matching controls in its event-study analysis.
